excerpts.text: The Chinese church must stand on its own feet. We need no foreign money, no foreign pastors, no foreign theology.
I was a cloth merchant in Beijing when the Holy Spirit fell upon me. I fasted for 39 days and received a divine commission.
The foreign missionaries mean well, but they have made Christianity a Western religion. We must make it Chinese.
I changed my name from Wei Enbo to Paul — like the apostle, I was called to plant churches among my own people.
Baptism must be face-down in living water. The Sabbath must be observed on Saturday. These are not Western customs but biblical commands.
In two years I planted churches across north China. The Holy Spirit moved faster than any missionary society.
The Western denominations divided Christ's body into a hundred pieces. We seek to restore the one true church.
I received the gift of tongues and the gift of healing. These are not past miracles but present realities.
The True Jesus Church accepts no foreign money. Every church is built by Chinese hands and Chinese offerings.
Before my conversion I was an ordinary man. After my conversion I was an instrument of God. The difference is the Holy Spirit.
We preach in the streets, in the markets, in the villages. The gospel is not for the educated elite — it is for all Chinese people.
I died at 42, only two years after founding the church. But the movement outlived me because it belonged to God, not to me.
The missionaries built schools and hospitals. Good. But they should have built Chinese leaders. That is what lasts.
When Chinese preach to Chinese in Chinese about a Chinese church, the gospel comes alive in ways no foreign missionary can achieve.
The True Jesus Church grew to 100,000 members by 1949 — without a single foreign missionary, without a single foreign dollar.
My illness did not shake my faith. I told my followers: the church does not depend on one man. It depends on the Holy Spirit.
